title: Quantified comparison predicate and NOT NOT gives wrong result
description:
Adding NOT in front of a quantified comparison predicate (e.g. v >ALL (<subquery>)
properly inverts the result (and makes it equivalent to v <ANY (<subquery>)).

However, extending this to NOT NOT gives a wrong result.
How to repeat:
CREATE TABLE it
 (pk    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  low   INTEGER NOT NULL,
  lown  INTEGER,
  mid   INTEGER NOT NULL,
  midn  INTEGER,
  hig   INTEGER NOT NULL,
  hign  INTEGER,
  nulls INTEGER,
  eql   INTEGER NOT NULL,
  eqln  INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE ot(
  v INTEGER NOT NULL,
  vn INTEGER);

INSERT INTO it(pk, low, lown, mid, midn, hig, hign, nulls, eql, eqln) VALUES
 (1, 1,    1, 4,    4, 7,    7, NULL, 11,    11),
 (2, 2,    2, 5,    5, 8,    8, NULL, 11,    11),
 (3, 3, NULL, 5, NULL, 9, NULL, NULL, 11, NULL);

INSERT INTO ot VALUES (1, 1), (2, NULL);

SELECT * FROM ot WHERE v >ALL (SELECT eql FROM it);
Empty set (0.00 sec)

SELECT * FROM ot WHERE not v >ALL (SELECT eql FROM it);
+---+------+
| v | vn   |
+---+------+
| 1 |    1 |
| 2 | NULL |
+---+------+
2 rows in set (0.00 sec)

SELECT * FROM ot WHERE not not v >ALL (SELECT eql FROM it);
+---+------+
| v | vn   |
+---+------+
| 1 |    1 |
| 2 | NULL |
+---+------+
2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
Suggested fix:
Fix the transformations.
poc:
CREATE TABLE it
(pk    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
low   INTEGER NOT NULL,
lown  INTEGER,
mid   INTEGER NOT NULL,
midn  INTEGER,
hig   INTEGER NOT NULL,
hign  INTEGER,
nulls INTEGER,
eql   INTEGER NOT NULL,
eqln  INTEGER
);

INSERT INTO it(pk, low, lown, mid, midn, hig, hign, nulls, eql, eqln) VALUES
(1, 1,    1, 4,    4, 7,    7, NULL, 11,    11),
(2, 2,    2, 5,    5, 8,    8, NULL, 11,    11),
(3, 3, NULL, 5, NULL, 9, NULL, NULL, 11, NULL);
SELECT * FROM ot WHERE v >ALL (SELECT eql FROM it);
SELECT * FROM ot WHERE not v >ALL (SELECT eql FROM it);
SELECT * FROM ot WHERE not not v >ALL (SELECT eql FROM it);
key_bug_features: NOT NOT with quantified comparison predicate gives wrong result
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value INTEGER NOT NULL, nullable_value INTEGER, check_value INTEGER CHECK (check_value > 0));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, nullable_value, check_value) VALUES (1, 5, NULL, 10), (2, 15, 20, 0), (3, 25, 30, 5);
CREATE TABLE reference_table (ref_value INTEGER NOT NULL, ref_nullable_value INTEGER);
INSERT INTO reference_table (ref_value, ref_nullable_value) VALUES (10, NULL), (20, 15), (30, 25);
SELECT * FROM reference_table WHERE NOT NOT ref_value > ALL (SELECT check_value FROM test_table WHERE check_value IS NOT NULL);
